title: Confidence Interval Visualizer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive simulation where students draw repeated samples from a fish population to build intuition about confidence intervals, margin of error, and sample size effects

learningObjectives: Interpret confidence intervals and margin of error in environmental measurements
Explain the relationship between sample size and confidence interval width
Apply understanding of statistical uncertainty to environmental regulatory decisions
prerequisites: Basic understanding of mean and variability
Familiarity with population vs. sample concepts

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Repeated sampling with visual accumulation of evidence
keyQuestions: Why do approximately 5% of 95% confidence intervals miss the true mean?
How does increasing sample size affect the width of confidence intervals?
What is the trade-off between confidence level and interval precision?
How should confidence intervals inform environmental safety decisions?
commonMisconceptions: Students often believe a 95% confidence interval means there is a 95% probability the true mean is inside that specific interval, rather than understanding it as a property of the sampling procedure
Many students think a wider confidence interval is always better because it is more likely to contain the true value, not recognizing the trade-off with precision
Students may assume that a single sample mean is the true population value without considering sampling variability
assessmentOpportunities: Students count how many of their 30 intervals miss the true mean and compare to the expected proportion
Students analyze a real environmental data scenario using confidence interval reasoning
Students explain the correct interpretation of a confidence interval vs. the common incorrect interpretation
